title: UX Widget Interface Version 2
text: UX Widget Interface Version 2 UX Widget Interface Version 2 introduces a new format for the data argument in the UX Widget Interface API functions: processLayout() , onConnect() , dataUpdate() , and dataCleanup() . This update simplifies the implementation of complex widgets with sub-widgets. The key changes in the new data argument format include: Different type of properties/data-internals: For example, properties such as html, style, class, value, and valrep are now contained in a single-level object, prefixed according to their category. Removal of the 'uniface:' prefix: Properties previously prefixed with uniface: no longer include this prefix. These changes significantly simplify the processing of data arguments in complex widgets with sub-widgets. For more information about the updated data argument structure, see the Widget API functions: processLayout , onConnect , dataUpdate , and dataCleanup . Related Links UX Widget Interface Version With UX Widget Interface Version, each UX widget receives an additional setting: uxInterfaceVersion in the physical widget definition section of the web.ini file. This setting specifies the version of the UX interface used to implement the widget. Migration to Interface Version 2 To work with Interface Version 2, each UX widget or your custom widget must include the uxInterfaceVersion = 2 setting in the physical widget definition section of the web.ini file. Developers must ensure this setting is added when implementing a custom widget with Interface Version 2. Compatibility Utility The Compatibility Utility enables you to make your custom widget, implemented using a previous UX Interface version, compatible with a newer UX Interface version. It is provided as a set of JavaScript modules located by default in the following directory: uniface/webapps/ux_compatibility_utils/ How to Use the Compatibility Utility The Compatibility Utility consists of JavaScript modules located in the default installation directory: uniface/webapps/ux_compatibility_utils/. This utility ensures compatibility of the UX Widget API's data arguments with the previous interface version. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
